ideation: This animation sequence depicts a woman in a motorized wheelchair moving to the right.  The animation focuses on the natural arm movements one might expect while operating such a device. It conveys a sense of control and movement. Frame 1 will show the woman's arms in a neutral position, as if she is just beginning her movement. Frame 2 will show her left arm slightly extended and her right arm slightly bent, as if she is gently controlling the joystick or steering mechanism. Frame 3 will show her moving her arms more actively, perhaps adjusting her position as she moves to the right. Frame 4 will show her returning to a slightly more neutral position but still indicating forward motion, and Frame 5 will show her arms returning to a neutral position, suggesting that she has completed the movement and is now steady.

frames.beat: arms rest at sides, ready to move
left arm extends, right arm guides forward
both arms actively steer the chair to the right
arms adjust, continuing the steady motion
arms return to rest, movement complete
